page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server instance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C x86_64 processor operating at 2.3 GHz, delivering 288 dedicated vCPUs across 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system features a massive 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix math. It is equipped with 2232 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 48 NVMe SSDs totaling 19031 GB of high-speed capacity. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth. It achieves a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 1060.25 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 411.36 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are exceptionally strong, with Redis operations reaching 390.97 million operations per second. Single-core performance is also highly competitive, ranking in the top tier for Geekbench single-core and the top quarter for stress-ng single-core tests. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speeds for smaller models represent a relative bottleneck, falling into the bottom quarter.

Given its dense allocation of 288 vCPUs, massive memory capacity, and 19 TB of local NVMe storage, this instance offers high resource density. The qualitative cost efficiency is optimal for enterprise-scale workloads that require simultaneous high-throughput memory access and massive local scratch space, avoiding the latency of network-attached storage. It is highly suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory data grids, heavy software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a high-density, memory-optimized server configured with 288 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6985P-C x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 2232 GB of RAM and 19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 48 drives, with no GPU acceleration. The architecture includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database execution speeds, though text generation for smaller language models is a relative bottleneck. This server is highly suited for massive in-memory databases, Redis deployments, large-scale software compilation, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd features 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2232 GB RAM, and 19031 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 288 vCPUs, 2232 GB RAM, and 19 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage server featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs and 19 TB of local NVMe SSDs for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with Intel AMX and AVX-512.
2232 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth.
19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 48 bundled drives.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database operations, and Redis performance.
Best suited for massive in-memory databases, caching layers, and heavy compilation.
